title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for authorization, object ownership, properties, functions, and tenancy and requires the smallest safe deny-by-default authorization decision matrix with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz team handles case 2081 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Competency probe: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
